message:
fix(runtime): treat closed join channel as terminal in TaskHandle::is_finished

is_finished() now returns true when the oneshot receiver is closed (not just
when a value is ready), preventing a race condition where dropping an unpolled
join future would stamp an abort reason onto a task that has already terminated
and closed its join channel. The two terminal states are now:
1. Value ready (normal completion)
2. Channel closed (task already finished, result consumed or dropped)

Updated doc comment to explain both terminal states. New test:
drop_join_does_not_abort_if_channel_already_closed verifies that dropping
the join handle after the task's channel is closed does not trigger an
erroneous abort.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
